features.description: The Newfoot single-page app loads at the root URL with the page title 'Newfoot' and no console errors.
steps: Navigate to the app root URL.
Assert the document title contains 'Newfoot'.
Assert the Angular app shell (header and main content area) is visible.
Check the browser console and assert there are no error-level messages.
Take a screenshot of the loaded home page.

features.description: Backend API is reachable through the frontend origin at /api so the whole app works over a single ngrok tunnel.
steps: Navigate to the app root URL.
Fetch '/api/health' from the same origin via the browser (e.g. window.fetch in page context).
Assert the response status is 200 and the JSON body contains a status field equal to 'ok'.
Assert no CORS error appears in the console.

features.description: Deep links survive a full page refresh: reloading any route (e.g. /wizard) re-renders that route instead of a 404.
steps: Navigate to the app root and click the CTA to open the wizard route.
Assert the URL path is '/wizard' (or equivalent) and wizard content is visible.
Trigger a full browser reload on that URL.
Assert the wizard page renders again (no server 404 and no blank page).

features.description: The dockerized stack serves the app end-to-end: frontend, backend, and database all up from one docker-compose environment.
steps: Navigate to the app URL exposed by the docker-compose stack.
Assert the home page renders.
Fetch '/api/health' and assert the JSON reports database connectivity as healthy.
Take a screenshot as evidence the full stack is serving.

features.description: The app is served over HTTPS through the ngrok tunnel and reports a secure context so camera APIs are available.
steps: Navigate to the ngrok HTTPS URL of the app.
In page context evaluate 'window.isSecureContext' and assert it is true.
Evaluate 'typeof navigator.mediaDevices?.getUserMedia' and assert it is 'function'.
Assert the browser address bar context is https (page URL starts with https://).

features.description: A distinctive typography system is applied: a branded display font for headings and a readable body font, loaded without FOUC fallback errors.
steps: Navigate to the home page.
Evaluate getComputedStyle on an h1 and on body text; assert the heading font-family differs from the default serif/sans stack and matches the brand font.
Assert font sizes follow a visible scale (h1 computed size > h2 > body).
Take a screenshot showing headings and body copy together.

features.description: A coherent brand color system is used across the UI: primary, accent, and semantic colors applied via CSS custom properties.
steps: Navigate to the home page.
Evaluate getComputedStyle(document.documentElement).getPropertyValue for '--color-primary' (or equivalent token) and assert it returns a non-empty color.
Assert the primary CTA button background matches the primary token color.
Take a screenshot of a screen showing primary and accent colors in use.

features.description: Buttons have distinct visual states: default, hover, focus-visible, and disabled are all visually differentiated.
steps: Navigate to a page with a primary button (e.g. home CTA).
Screenshot the button at rest, then hover it and screenshot again; assert the two screenshots differ (background or elevation change).
Focus the button via keyboard Tab and assert a visible focus ring is present.
Locate a disabled button (e.g. wizard 'Continue' before capture) and assert it has reduced opacity or disabled styling and does not respond to click.

features.description: Card components with consistent radius, elevation, and padding are used for report items, view selection, and insole zones.
steps: Navigate to the wizard view-selection screen.
Assert the three view cards share identical computed border-radius and padding.
Navigate to a deficiency report and assert report items use the same card treatment.
Take a screenshot of both screens for visual comparison.

features.description: Purposeful motion: route transitions and card entrances animate smoothly (fade/slide) rather than popping in.
steps: Navigate from the home page to the wizard.
Assert a CSS transition or animation is applied to the entering view (check computed animation/transition properties or capture two frames showing intermediate opacity).
Assert the animation completes within ~500ms and the page is fully interactive afterwards.
Take a screenshot of the settled page.

features.description: Form inputs (text, select, radio) share consistent branded styling with clear labels and focus states.
steps: Navigate to the foot size form.
Assert each input has a visible associated label.
Focus an input and assert its border/outline changes to the brand focus style.
Take a screenshot of the styled form.

features.description: A toast/snackbar component surfaces transient feedback (e.g. 'Analysis saved') with auto-dismiss.
steps: Complete an action that saves data (e.g. accept a captured view so it persists to the backend).
Assert a toast appears with a confirmation message.
Wait up to 6 seconds and assert the toast auto-dismisses.
Take a screenshot while the toast is visible.

features.description: A consistent spacing rhythm is applied: page sections use a spacing scale so screens do not look cramped or uneven.
steps: Navigate to the home page and to the report page.
Evaluate computed margins/gaps between main sections and assert they use consistent step values (e.g. multiples of a base unit like 8px).
Take full-page screenshots of both pages and visually confirm even vertical rhythm.

features.description: The Newfoot logo renders as an inline SVG in the app header on every page.
steps: Navigate to the home page and assert an <svg> logo element is present inside the header.
Navigate to the wizard and the insole page and assert the same header logo is present.
Assert the SVG has an accessible name (title element or aria-label containing 'Newfoot').
Take a screenshot of the header.

features.description: The favicon is the Newfoot mark served as SVG and appears in the browser tab.
steps: Navigate to the app root.
Assert a <link rel='icon'> pointing to an .svg asset exists in the document head.
Fetch the favicon URL and assert it returns 200 with an SVG content type.
Take a screenshot including the browser tab strip if available.

features.description: Clicking the header logo navigates back to the home page from anywhere in the app.
steps: Navigate to the deficiency report page (or any deep route).
Click the header logo.
Assert the URL is the root and the home hero is visible.

features.description: The home page hero presents the brand: logo lockup, product name, one-line value proposition, and primary CTA.
steps: Navigate to the home page.
Assert the hero shows the Newfoot name, a tagline describing posture analysis and insoles, and a 'Start' CTA button.
Take a screenshot of the hero.

features.description: The logo remains legible in both light and dark themes (adapts color or uses a theme-safe variant).
steps: Navigate to the home page in light mode and screenshot the header logo.
Switch to dark mode via the theme toggle.
Assert the logo is still visible with sufficient contrast against the dark header (its fill differs from the header background).
Screenshot the dark-mode header.

features.description: A sticky app header stays visible while scrolling and contains logo, navigation, and theme toggle.
steps: Navigate to a long page (e.g. full report).
Scroll to the bottom of the page.
Assert the header is still visible at the top of the viewport (position sticky/fixed).
Assert logo, nav links, and theme toggle are inside the header.

features.description: Primary navigation links reach the three core areas: Posture Scan, My Insole, and About/Disclaimer.
steps: Navigate to the home page.
Click the 'Posture Scan' nav item and assert the wizard route renders.
Click the 'My Insole' nav item and assert the insole area renders.
Click the 'About' or 'Disclaimer' nav item and assert that page renders.

features.description: The active navigation item is visually highlighted to reflect the current route.
steps: Navigate to the wizard route.
Assert the 'Posture Scan' nav item has an active style (e.g. aria-current='page' or distinct class/color).
Navigate to the insole route and assert the highlight moves accordingly.

features.description: A footer on every page contains the medical disclaimer link, an about link, and the app version.
steps: Navigate to the home page and scroll to the footer.
Assert the footer contains a link to the full disclaimer page and a visible version string.
Repeat on the report page and assert the same footer renders.

features.description: Unknown routes show a branded 404 page with a link back home.
steps: Navigate directly to '/this-route-does-not-exist'.
Assert a styled 'Page not found' screen renders (not a blank page or raw server error).
Click the 'Back to home' link and assert the home page loads.

features.description: Browser back/forward navigation works through the wizard steps without losing captured progress.
steps: Start the wizard, complete the FRONT view capture, and continue to the SIDE view.
Press the browser Back button and assert the app returns to the previous wizard state without a full reload.
Press Forward and assert the SIDE step is restored.
Assert the FRONT view still shows as completed in the progress indicator.

features.description: The home page shows a clear two-step journey overview: '1. Scan your posture' then '2. Get your insole', each linking to its flow.
steps: Navigate to the home page.
Assert two journey cards/sections are visible describing the scan step and the insole step.
Click the scan card and assert the wizard opens; go back and click the insole card and assert the insole area opens.

features.description: Opening a wizard capture step requests camera access and shows the live preview once granted.
steps: Launch the browser with the fake-camera test video (e.g. --use-fake-device-for-media-stream and --use-fake-ui-for-media-stream with the test file).
Navigate to the wizard FRONT capture step.
Assert a <video> element becomes visible and is playing (readyState >= 2, videoWidth > 0).
Take a screenshot showing the live preview.

features.description: While the camera initializes, a loading indicator with a 'Starting camera…' message is shown instead of a black box.
steps: Navigate to the wizard capture step with the fake camera enabled.
Immediately assert a spinner or skeleton with camera-starting copy is visible before the stream is ready.
Wait for the preview and assert the loading indicator is replaced by the live video.

features.description: The camera preview is mirrored for front-facing use, with a mirror toggle to flip it.
steps: Open the capture step with the fake camera.
Assert the video element has a horizontal mirror transform applied by default.
Click the mirror toggle and assert the transform is removed.
Take screenshots in both states.

features.description: A camera-facing toggle lets the user switch between front and rear camera (facingMode), keeping the preview running.
steps: Open the capture step with the fake camera.
Assert a camera-switch button is visible.
Click it and assert the preview restarts and continues playing (videoWidth > 0 after switch).
Assert no unhandled error toast appears.

features.description: Leaving the capture screen stops the camera stream (no lingering recording).
steps: Open the capture step with the fake camera and confirm the preview is playing.
Navigate back to the home page.
In page context assert the previous MediaStream tracks report readyState 'ended' (app exposes this via the video element or a debug hook).
Assert no <video> element with an active stream remains in the DOM.

features.description: The preview maintains correct aspect ratio without distortion in both portrait and landscape viewports.
steps: Open the capture step with the fake camera at a desktop viewport and assert the video is not stretched (element aspect ratio matches videoWidth/videoHeight).
Resize the viewport to 390x844 (phone portrait).
Assert the preview letterboxes or crops gracefully with no distortion and controls remain visible.
Screenshot both viewports.

features.description: On plain http (insecure context) the capture step shows a clear explanation that the camera requires HTTPS, with the ngrok URL hint, instead of failing silently.
steps: Navigate to the app over http:// (insecure origin) if available, or simulate isSecureContext=false.
Open the wizard capture step.
Assert a friendly message explains the camera needs a secure (HTTPS) connection and no broken video element is shown.
Take a screenshot of the message.

features.description: A capture-quality hint bar tells the user to step back until their full body is in frame.
steps: Open the FRONT capture step with the fake-camera test video.
Assert a hint message about framing the full body is visible above or below the preview.
Assert the hint updates or disappears once a full-body pose is detected in the test video.
Screenshot the hint state.

features.description: The MediaPipe Pose Landmarker loads client-side with a visible model-loading progress state before detection starts.
steps: Open the wizard capture step with the fake camera.
Assert a 'Loading pose model…' indicator appears while the model initializes.
Wait until the indicator resolves and assert a 'Pose engine ready' state or the skeleton overlay becomes active.
Assert no console errors related to model loading.

features.description: A real-time skeleton overlay (33 landmarks with connecting bones) is drawn over the live preview.
steps: Open the capture step with the fake-camera test video showing a person.
Wait for detection and assert an overlay canvas is rendered above the video.
In page context assert the app reports 33 detected landmarks (via a data attribute or debug hook).
Take a screenshot showing the skeleton drawn over the body.

features.description: The skeleton overlay tracks movement live, updating in real time as the subject moves in the test video.
steps: Open the capture step with a fake-camera test video containing motion.
Capture the overlay canvas pixels at two timestamps 2 seconds apart.
Assert the two captures differ, proving the overlay updates with movement.
Assert the UI remains responsive (a button click registers) while tracking.

features.description: A pose-detection status badge shows 'Pose detected' (green) or 'No pose' (amber) in real time.
steps: Open the capture step with the fake-camera test video of a person and assert the badge shows a detected state.
Switch to a fake video (or cover) with no person and assert the badge flips to a no-pose state.
Screenshot both badge states.

features.description: Low-confidence landmarks are visually de-emphasized (dimmed or dashed) so users see which body parts are unreliable.
steps: Open the capture step with a test video where part of the body is out of frame.
Assert landmarks below the confidence threshold render with a visibly different style than confident ones.
Take a screenshot showing both styles.

features.description: A live FPS/performance readout is available on the capture screen showing the pose pipeline frame rate.
steps: Open the capture step with the fake camera.
Assert an FPS counter (or performance chip) is visible and shows a number greater than 0.
Wait 3 seconds and assert the value updates.
Screenshot the readout.

features.description: The captured frame stores 3D landmark data, and the report can show a 'landmarks captured: 33 (3D)' confirmation detail.
steps: Complete a FRONT capture with the fake-camera test video.
Open the per-view report or its details section.
Assert a detail line confirms 33 landmarks with 3D coordinates were captured for the analysis.
Screenshot the confirmation.

features.description: The wizard entry screen presents three view cards — FRONT, SIDE, BACK — each with an illustration and status.
steps: Navigate to the wizard entry route.
Assert three cards labeled FRONT, SIDE, and BACK are visible, each with an illustration and a 'Not captured' status.
Take a screenshot of the entry screen.

features.description: Selecting the FRONT view opens the FRONT capture flow with view-specific instructions.
steps: From the wizard entry, click the FRONT card.
Assert the capture screen opens showing 'Front view' in the step heading.
Assert instructions mention facing the camera straight on.
Screenshot the instruction state.

features.description: A human silhouette alignment guide overlays the camera preview so users can position themselves correctly for the selected view.
steps: Open the FRONT capture step with the fake camera.
Assert a semi-transparent silhouette outline overlay is rendered over the preview.
Switch to the SIDE view capture and assert the silhouette changes to a profile outline.
Screenshot both silhouettes.

features.description: A countdown capture mode counts down 3-2-1 with large on-screen numbers and then freezes the captured frame.
steps: Open the FRONT capture step with the fake camera and click 'Capture with countdown'.
Assert large countdown digits appear and change each second.
After the countdown, assert the preview freezes into a captured still frame.
Screenshot the frozen frame.

features.description: A live analysis mode continuously analyzes posture without capturing, showing metrics update in real time.
steps: Open the capture step with the fake camera and switch on 'Live mode'.
Assert live posture metrics (e.g. shoulder tilt angle) are displayed and updating while the video plays.
Assert no capture is stored (progress still shows the view as not captured).
Switch live mode off and assert the metrics panel returns to idle.

features.description: After capture, the frozen frame shows the skeleton overlay baked onto it for review.
steps: Complete a countdown capture on the FRONT view with the fake camera.
Assert the still frame displays the detected skeleton overlay on the body.
Screenshot the reviewed capture.

features.description: A 'Retake' button on the capture review discards the frame and returns to the live preview.
steps: Complete a FRONT capture and land on the review state.
Click 'Retake'.
Assert the live camera preview resumes and the previous still is gone.
Assert the view status remains 'Not captured' until accepted.

features.description: An 'Accept' action on the capture review saves the view analysis and advances the wizard.
steps: Complete a FRONT capture and click 'Accept'.
Assert a saving confirmation appears and the wizard advances (next step or per-view report).
Assert the FRONT view now shows a completed status in the progress indicator.

features.description: The SIDE view flow works end-to-end: profile silhouette, capture, review, accept.
steps: From the wizard, select the SIDE view.
Assert the profile silhouette guide and side-specific instructions are shown.
Complete a countdown capture with the fake-camera test video, then accept it.
Assert SIDE shows as completed in the wizard progress.

features.description: The BACK view flow works end-to-end: rear silhouette, capture, review, accept.
steps: From the wizard, select the BACK view.
Assert back-view instructions (facing away from camera) and silhouette are shown.
Complete a capture with the fake-camera test video and accept it.
Assert BACK shows as completed in the wizard progress.

features.description: A persistent step progress indicator shows capture state across the three views (e.g. 2 of 3 captured).
steps: Complete FRONT and SIDE captures.
Assert the progress indicator shows those two views completed and BACK pending, with a '2/3' style summary.
Screenshot the progress indicator.

features.description: Completing all three views lands on a completion screen summarizing the scan and offering 'View full report' and 'Get my insole' actions.
steps: Complete FRONT, SIDE, and BACK captures with the fake camera.
Assert a completion screen appears congratulating the user with all three views marked done.
Assert buttons 'View full report' and 'Get my insole' are visible and navigate correctly when clicked.
Screenshot the completion screen.

features.description: The FRONT report shows the shoulder tilt angle in degrees with a normal/deviated classification.
steps: Complete a FRONT capture with the fake-camera test video and open the FRONT report.
Assert a 'Shoulder tilt' metric row displays a numeric angle with a degree unit.
Assert a classification badge (e.g. Normal / Mild / Marked) is shown next to the value.
Screenshot the metric row.

features.description: The FRONT report shows the pelvic tilt (iliac/hip line) angle with classification.
steps: Open the FRONT report after capture.
Assert a 'Pelvic tilt' metric shows a numeric degree value and a threshold-based badge.
Screenshot the metric.

features.description: The FRONT report shows head lateral lean relative to vertical with classification.
steps: Open the FRONT report after capture.
Assert a 'Head lean' metric displays degrees from vertical with a classification badge.
Screenshot the metric.

features.description: The FRONT report shows knee alignment (valgus/varus) per leg with classification.
steps: Open the FRONT report after capture.
Assert knee alignment values are shown for left and right legs, each labeled valgus, varus, or neutral.
Screenshot the knee metrics.

features.description: The SIDE report shows the craniovertebral (forward-head) angle with classification.
steps: Complete a SIDE capture with the fake camera and open the SIDE report.
Assert a 'Forward head' metric shows the craniovertebral angle in degrees with a badge.
Screenshot the metric.

features.description: The SIDE report shows thoracic slump (rounded upper back) with classification.
steps: Open the SIDE report after capture.
Assert a 'Thoracic slump' metric displays a value with a classification badge.
Screenshot the metric.

features.description: The BACK report shows spine lateral deviation with classification.
steps: Complete a BACK capture with the fake camera and open the BACK report.
Assert a 'Spine lateral deviation' metric shows a value and badge.
Screenshot the metric.

features.description: The BACK report shows rearfoot pronation/supination per foot with classification.
steps: Open the BACK report after capture.
Assert rearfoot alignment values for left and right feet are labeled pronation, supination, or neutral.
Screenshot the foot metrics.

features.description: Each metric row exposes its threshold on demand (tooltip or expander) so users see why a value is classified as deviated.
steps: Open any per-view report.
Click the info affordance on a metric row.
Assert the threshold range (e.g. 'Normal: below 2°') is displayed.
Screenshot the expanded explanation.

features.description: Metric severity uses consistent color coding across all views (e.g. green normal, amber mild, red marked).
steps: Open the FRONT and SIDE reports.
Assert badges of the same classification level share the same color across both reports.
Assert at least two different severity colors are distinguishable in the test data.
Screenshot both reports for comparison.

features.description: After accepting a capture, a per-view deficiency report renders with the annotated capture image and a metrics list.
steps: Accept a FRONT capture with the fake camera.
Assert the report screen shows the captured photo thumbnail and a list of posture metrics beneath or beside it.
Screenshot the full report.

features.description: Visual annotations are drawn on the captured image: angle lines/arcs at the relevant joints for each measured metric.
steps: Open the FRONT report.
Assert annotation graphics (lines across shoulders and hips, angle arcs) are overlaid on the capture image.
Assert each annotation is color-matched to its metric severity.
Screenshot the annotated image.

features.description: Every detected deficiency includes a plain-language explanation of what it means in everyday terms.
steps: Open a report containing at least one deviated metric.
Expand or read the deficiency item and assert a jargon-free sentence explains the finding (e.g. 'Your left shoulder sits slightly higher than your right').
Assert the explanation avoids raw metric jargon as the only description.
Screenshot the explanation.

features.description: A per-view summary header states the count of findings (e.g. '2 findings in front view').
steps: Open the FRONT report after capture.
Assert a summary line states how many deficiencies were detected in that view.
Screenshot the header.

features.description: When all metrics are within normal thresholds, the report shows a positive 'No significant deviations detected' state.
steps: Complete a capture using a fake-camera test video of a well-aligned pose.
Open the per-view report.
Assert a positive all-clear message with a success visual is displayed instead of an empty findings list.
Screenshot the state.

features.description: A combined full report aggregates findings from all captured views on one screen, grouped by view.
steps: Complete all three view captures.
Open the full report.
Assert sections for FRONT, SIDE, and BACK each list their metrics and findings.
Assert an overall summary (total findings across views) is shown at the top.
Screenshot the full report.

features.description: Each deficiency item shows a recognizable icon for the affected body area (shoulders, pelvis, head, knees, spine, feet).
steps: Open a report with multiple findings.
Assert each finding row includes a distinct body-area icon.
Screenshot the findings list.

features.description: The full report is shareable/revisitable via URL: reloading the report page restores it for the current session.
steps: Open the full report after completing captures.
Copy the current URL and perform a full page reload.
Assert the same report content renders after reload (data restored via session).
Screenshot the restored report.

features.description: The capture review overlays a reference-posture 'ghost' skeleton on top of the user's detected skeleton.
steps: Complete a FRONT capture with the fake camera.
Assert two skeletons render on the still: the user's skeleton and a visually distinct ghost (dashed or translucent) representing ideal posture.
Screenshot the overlay.

features.description: The ghost skeleton is scaled and anchored to the user's body size and position so the comparison is meaningful.
steps: Open the capture review with the ghost visible.
Assert the ghost's hip midpoint aligns with the user's hip midpoint and the ghost height approximately matches the user skeleton height (evaluate overlay geometry via debug hook or pixel positions).
Screenshot the aligned comparison.

features.description: A toggle shows/hides the reference ghost on the capture review and report screens.
steps: Open the capture review.
Click the 'Show reference' toggle off and assert the ghost disappears while the user skeleton remains.
Toggle it back on and assert the ghost returns.
Screenshot both states.

features.description: Body segments deviating beyond threshold are highlighted in a warning color on the user skeleton, directly showing the difference from the reference.
steps: Open a capture review with at least one deviated metric.
Assert the corresponding skeleton segment (e.g. shoulder line) is rendered in the warning color while normal segments stay neutral.
Screenshot the highlighted deviation.

features.description: A side-by-side comparison mode shows 'You' and 'Reference' panels together for the selected view.
steps: From the report, activate the side-by-side comparison mode.
Assert two labeled panels render: the user's annotated capture and the reference posture illustration.
Screenshot the side-by-side view.

features.description: The insole flow starts with a foot size form asking for shoe size with an EU/US unit selector.
steps: Navigate to the insole section after completing the scan.
Assert a form with a numeric shoe-size input and an EU/US unit toggle is visible.
Screenshot the form.

features.description: Switching between EU and US converts the entered size to the equivalent value automatically.
steps: On the foot size form, enter 42 with EU selected.
Switch the unit toggle to US.
Assert the displayed size converts to the corresponding US value (approximately 8.5–9).
Switch back to EU and assert it returns to 42.

features.description: The form validates size bounds and shows an inline error for out-of-range values while disabling submit.
steps: Enter an out-of-range size such as 99 (EU).
Assert an inline validation message appears explaining the accepted range.
Assert the 'Generate insole' button is disabled.
Enter 42 and assert the error clears and the button enables.

features.description: The form shows a read-only summary of the posture findings that will drive the insole recommendation.
steps: Complete the scan, then open the insole form.
Assert a summary panel lists the relevant detected deficiencies (e.g. rearfoot pronation, pelvic tilt) feeding the recommendation.
Screenshot the summary.

features.description: An optional foot-length field (in mm/cm) refines sizing, with its own validation.
steps: On the insole form, enter a foot length of 265 mm.
Assert the value is accepted; then enter 500 and assert a range validation error appears.
Correct to 265 and assert the form is submittable.

features.description: Submitting the form triggers insole generation with a progress state, then navigates to the insole result.
steps: Fill the form with EU 42 and submit.
Assert a generating/progress indicator appears.
Assert the app navigates to the insole result screen showing the generated recommendation.
Screenshot the result landing.

features.description: Form values persist for the session: reloading the page restores the previously entered size and unit.
steps: Enter EU 42 on the insole form.
Reload the page.
Assert the form shows EU 42 restored.
Assert the unit toggle reflects the saved selection.

features.description: Detected rearfoot pronation produces an insole with visible medial arch support, and the UI states this mapping.
steps: Complete a scan whose BACK analysis (fake-camera test video) yields pronation, then generate the insole.
Assert the recommendation lists a 'medial arch support' feature.
Assert the rationale text links it to the detected pronation.
Screenshot the recommendation.

features.description: Detected supination produces lateral support/posting in the recommendation with matching rationale.
steps: Generate an insole from an analysis classified as supination (use a matching test capture or seeded analysis).
Assert the recommendation includes lateral wedge/support.
Assert the rationale references supination.
Screenshot the recommendation.

features.description: Pelvic tilt findings produce a heel-lift element on the appropriate side, explained in plain language.
steps: Generate an insole from an analysis with pelvic tilt beyond threshold.
Assert the recommendation includes a heel lift with the affected side named.
Assert the explanation states it addresses the measured pelvic tilt.
Screenshot the recommendation.

features.description: The generated insole is broken down into named zones (heel cup, arch support, metatarsal area, forefoot) each with its parameter values.
steps: Open the insole result screen.
Assert a zone list shows at least heel, arch, and forefoot zones with numeric parameters (e.g. arch height in mm, heel cup depth in mm).
Screenshot the zone breakdown.

features.description: The insole dimensions are parametric to the entered foot size: changing the size changes the reported insole length.
steps: Generate an insole at EU 42 and note the displayed insole length.
Return to the form, change to EU 44, and regenerate.
Assert the displayed insole length increases accordingly.
Screenshot both results.

features.description: A neutral analysis (no relevant deficiencies) yields a comfort/neutral insole recommendation, clearly labeled as such.
steps: Generate an insole from an all-normal analysis (well-aligned test video).
Assert the result is labeled a neutral/comfort insole with no corrective posting.
Assert the rationale explains that no significant deviations were found.
Screenshot the neutral result.

features.description: The recommendation screen shows a 'Why this insole' section tracing each insole feature back to a specific measured finding.
steps: Open the insole result for an analysis with findings.
Assert a rationale section maps each corrective feature to the specific metric and angle that triggered it.
Screenshot the rationale section.

features.description: The generated insole renders as an interactive 3D model in a Three.js viewer on the result screen.
steps: Generate an insole and open the result screen.
Assert a WebGL canvas is present and a 3D insole mesh is visible (canvas has non-blank pixels).
Assert no WebGL errors in the console.
Screenshot the 3D viewer.

features.description: The 3D model rotates via mouse drag (orbit controls).
steps: Open the 3D viewer.
Capture the canvas pixels, then perform a mouse drag across the canvas.
Capture again and assert the rendering changed, proving rotation.
Screenshot the rotated model.

features.description: The 3D view zooms via scroll wheel (and pinch on touch), constrained to sensible limits.
steps: Open the 3D viewer and capture the canvas.
Dispatch wheel events to zoom in and assert the render changes (model appears larger).
Continue zooming past the limit and assert the model never clips through or disappears.
Screenshot the zoomed state.

features.description: An exploded-view toggle separates the insole zones vertically with an animated transition.
steps: Open the 3D viewer.
Click the 'Exploded view' toggle.
Assert the zones separate visibly (canvas render changes to show layered parts).
Toggle off and assert the model reassembles.
Screenshot the exploded state.

features.description: Zones in the 3D model are color-coded with a matching legend naming each zone.
steps: Open the 3D viewer with exploded view on.
Assert a legend lists zone names with color swatches.
Assert the legend colors correspond to distinct colors visible in the render.
Screenshot legend plus model.

features.description: A left/right selector switches the viewer between the left insole and the mirrored right insole.
steps: Open the 3D viewer showing the left insole.
Click the 'Right' toggle.
Assert the rendered model mirrors (capture before/after and assert the change) and the label updates to 'Right'.
Screenshot both sides.

features.description: A 'Reset view' button returns the camera to the default framing after any rotation/zoom.
steps: Open the 3D viewer, rotate and zoom the model.
Click 'Reset view'.
Assert the render returns to the initial default framing (canvas matches the initial capture within tolerance).
Screenshot the reset state.

features.description: While the 3D mesh is being prepared, the viewer shows a branded loading state instead of an empty canvas.
steps: Trigger insole generation and immediately open the result screen.
Assert a loading spinner/skeleton occupies the viewer area before the mesh appears.
Assert it is replaced by the rendered model once ready.
Screenshot the loading state.

features.description: A 'Download left insole (STL)' button downloads a non-empty .stl file named for the left side.
steps: Open the insole result screen.
Click the left STL download button.
Assert a file download completes with an .stl extension and 'left' in the filename.
Assert the downloaded file size is greater than 10 KB.

features.description: A 'Download right insole (STL)' button downloads the mirrored right-side STL.
steps: Open the insole result screen.
Click the right STL download button.
Assert a .stl file downloads with 'right' in the filename and non-trivial size.
Assert the UI labels it as mirrored from the left model.

features.description: The exported files are valid binary STL: the app surfaces a validation badge (triangle count, binary format) for each export.
steps: Open the insole result screen.
Assert each STL entry shows a validation summary such as 'Binary STL · N triangles · watertight'.
Download the left STL and assert its byte length equals 84 + 50 × triangle count as displayed.
Screenshot the validation badges.

features.description: The UI confirms the meshes are watertight (manifold) so they will slice correctly, with a green check per side.
steps: Open the insole result screen.
Assert watertight status indicators are shown for both left and right models and read as passing.
Screenshot the indicators.

features.description: A 'Download both (ZIP)' action packages left and right STLs plus the recommendation sheet in one archive.
steps: Click 'Download both' on the insole result screen.
Assert a .zip file downloads.
Assert the UI describes the archive contents (left STL, right STL, recommendation PDF/sheet).
Assert the archive size exceeds the size of a single STL.

features.description: Exported filenames encode the size and session for traceability (e.g. newfoot-left-eu42-<session>.stl).
steps: Download the left STL after generating an EU 42 insole.
Assert the filename contains 'left' and '42'.
Assert the filename contains a session-derived identifier.

features.description: STL downloads work on mobile viewport with an explanatory note about opening the file in a slicer.
steps: Set the viewport to 390x844 and open the insole result.
Assert the download buttons are visible and tappable without horizontal scrolling.
Tap the left STL button and assert the download triggers.
Assert helper text explains the file is for 3D printing slicers.

features.description: A printable one-page recommendation sheet is available from the insole result, opening in a clean print-ready layout.
steps: On the insole result screen, click 'Print sheet' or 'View recommendation sheet'.
Assert a one-page formatted sheet renders with the Newfoot branding.
Screenshot the sheet.

features.description: The sheet lists complete print settings: filament material, infill percentage, layer height, and print orientation.
steps: Open the recommendation sheet.
Assert values are present for material (e.g. TPU), infill %, layer height in mm, and orientation guidance.
Screenshot the print-settings block.

features.description: The sheet summarizes the insole parameters and the posture findings that drove them.
steps: Open the recommendation sheet after generating a corrective insole.
Assert the sheet lists foot size, key zone parameters, and the findings summary.
Screenshot the summary block.

features.description: Printing the sheet uses a dedicated print stylesheet: no header/nav chrome, fits on one page.
steps: Open the recommendation sheet and emulate print media (or trigger window.print with print emulation).
Assert app navigation and buttons are hidden in the print rendering.
Assert the content height fits a single A4/Letter page in the print preview emulation.
Screenshot the print-emulated view.

features.description: The medical disclaimer is printed on the recommendation sheet itself.
steps: Open the recommendation sheet.
Assert the 'Not medical advice — consult a healthcare professional' text is present on the sheet body (also under print emulation).
Screenshot showing the disclaimer on the sheet.

features.description: On first visit an anonymous session ID is generated and stored client-side, with no login ever requested.
steps: Open the app in a fresh browser context.
Assert no login/signup UI is presented anywhere in the primary flows.
Evaluate localStorage and assert a session ID key exists with a UUID-like value.
Reload and assert the value is unchanged.

features.description: Accepted captures are persisted to the backend keyed by the session ID (observable as a successful API write).
steps: Accept a FRONT capture with the fake camera while recording network requests.
Assert a POST/PUT to an /api analysis endpoint occurred with a 2xx response.
Assert the request payload or URL includes the session ID from localStorage.

features.description: Reloading the browser restores wizard progress and reports from the backend for the same session.
steps: Complete FRONT and SIDE captures, then fully reload the app.
Navigate to the wizard and assert FRONT and SIDE still show as completed.
Open the report and assert the persisted metrics render.
Screenshot the restored state.

features.description: A returning visitor sees a 'Welcome back' resume card on the home page linking to their existing analysis.
steps: Complete at least one capture, reload, and return to the home page.
Assert a resume card shows the previous scan status (e.g. '2 of 3 views captured') with a 'Continue' action.
Click Continue and assert the wizard opens at the right place.
Screenshot the resume card.

features.description: A 'Start over' action clears the current analysis after a confirmation dialog and begins a fresh session state.
steps: With an existing analysis, click 'Start over'.
Assert a confirmation dialog warns that current results will be cleared.
Confirm, then assert the wizard shows all three views as not captured and the report is empty.
Assert prior data no longer renders after reload.

features.description: The session ID is viewable on an About/Support panel so users can reference their anonymous analysis.
steps: Open the About page or settings panel.
Assert the anonymous session ID is displayed (possibly truncated with a copy button).
Click copy and assert a copied confirmation toast appears.

features.description: GET /api/health returns service status including database connectivity, consumable from the browser.
steps: From the app origin fetch '/api/health'.
Assert status 200 and JSON containing overall status and a database status field.
Assert response time is under 2 seconds.

features.description: POST /api/sessions/:id/analyses stores a per-view analysis and returns 201 with the stored record.
steps: Accept a capture in the UI while recording network traffic.
Assert the create request returned 201 (or 200) and the response JSON echoes the view name and metrics.
Assert the response contains a server-generated record id and timestamp.

features.description: GET /api/sessions/:id/analyses returns all stored view analyses for the session, powering report restore.
steps: After accepting two captures, fetch the session analyses endpoint from the page context using the stored session ID.
Assert the JSON array contains entries for both captured views with their metric values.
Assert unknown session IDs return an empty list or 404 JSON, not an HTML error page.

features.description: POST /api/insoles generates the parametric insole recommendation from analysis + foot size and returns the parameter set.
steps: Submit the insole form while recording network traffic.
Assert an insole generation request returned 2xx with JSON containing zone parameters (arch height, heel cup depth) and rationale codes.
Assert the returned parameters match the values displayed in the UI.

features.description: GET /api/insoles/:id/stl?side=left|right streams a binary STL with correct content type and attachment filename.
steps: Trigger the left STL download while recording network traffic.
Assert the STL request responded 200 with content-type 'model/stl' or 'application/octet-stream'.
Assert a Content-Disposition attachment filename ending in .stl is present.
Assert the right-side request returns a different (mirrored) body of similar size.

features.description: API errors return structured JSON (statusCode, message) that the UI renders as friendly toasts, never raw stack traces.
steps: From the page context, fetch an invalid API call (e.g. POST insole with size 999).
Assert the response is JSON with statusCode and a human-readable message and no stack trace.
Trigger the same validation through the UI and assert a friendly error toast appears.
Screenshot the toast.

features.description: Analyses persist durably in MySQL: data saved in one browser session is retrievable in a brand-new browser context using the same session ID.
steps: Complete a capture, then read the session ID from localStorage.
Open a fresh incognito context, set the same session ID into localStorage, and load the app.
Assert the previous analysis and wizard progress render in the new context.
Screenshot the restored data.

features.description: The report shows the capture timestamp for each view, sourced from the stored records.
steps: Complete a FRONT capture and open the report.
Assert a human-readable date/time is displayed for the FRONT capture.
Reload and assert the timestamp is identical (stored, not regenerated).

features.description: Re-capturing a view updates the stored analysis: the report reflects the latest capture, not duplicates.
steps: Complete a FRONT capture, then redo the FRONT view and accept a new capture.
Open the report and assert exactly one FRONT section is shown with the newest timestamp.
Fetch the session analyses API and assert only the latest FRONT analysis is returned as current.

features.description: Generated insole records are stored and retrievable: revisiting 'My Insole' after reload shows the previously generated recommendation without regenerating.
steps: Generate an insole, then fully reload the app.
Navigate to 'My Insole' and assert the same recommendation (same parameters and timestamp) renders from storage.
Assert no new generation request is fired on load (check network log).

features.description: Every analysis/report screen displays the disclaimer 'Not medical advice — consult a healthcare professional'.
steps: Open a per-view report and assert the disclaimer text is visible without scrolling hacks (present on the page).
Open the combined full report and assert the disclaimer is present there too.
Screenshot both placements.

features.description: The insole recommendation and 3D result screens display the same medical disclaimer.
steps: Open the insole form and assert the disclaimer is visible.
Open the insole result / 3D screen and assert the disclaimer is visible.
Screenshot both.

features.description: A dedicated disclaimer/about page explains app limitations (POC, estimation-based, not diagnostic) reachable from the footer.
steps: Click the disclaimer link in the footer.
Assert a full page describes the POC nature, measurement limitations, and the consult-a-professional guidance.
Screenshot the page.

features.description: The disclaimer banner is persistent (not dismissible) on analysis and recommendation screens.
steps: Open the report screen.
Assert the disclaimer element has no close/dismiss control.
Reload and assert it is still displayed.
Repeat on the insole result screen.

features.description: The home page renders a clean single-column layout at phone portrait (390x844) with no horizontal scroll.
steps: Set the viewport to 390x844 and load the home page.
Assert document.scrollingElement.scrollWidth <= viewport width (no horizontal overflow).
Assert the hero, journey cards, and CTA stack vertically.
Screenshot the mobile home page.

features.description: The wizard capture screen fits phone portrait: preview, silhouette, and capture controls all visible without scrolling.
steps: Set viewport to 390x844 and open the FRONT capture step with the fake camera.
Assert the video preview, capture button, and mode toggles are all within the viewport.
Screenshot the mobile capture layout.

features.description: Report metric cards stack in a single column on mobile and switch to a multi-column grid on desktop.
steps: Open the full report at 390x844 and assert metric cards are stacked one per row.
Resize to 1280x800 and assert the cards flow into two or more columns.
Screenshot both layouts.

features.description: The 3D insole viewer supports touch: one-finger drag rotates and two-finger pinch zooms on a touch viewport.
steps: Open the insole result at a touch-emulated mobile viewport.
Dispatch a one-finger drag on the canvas and assert the render changes (rotation).
Dispatch a pinch gesture and assert the model scale changes.
Screenshot the result.

features.description: On narrow screens the header collapses to a compact bar with a menu button revealing the navigation.
steps: Set viewport to 390x844 and load any page.
Assert the full nav links are hidden and a menu (hamburger) button is visible.
Tap the menu button and assert the navigation drawer/sheet opens with all nav items.
Screenshot the open menu.

features.description: All primary tap targets on mobile are at least 44x44 CSS pixels.
steps: At 390x844, open the wizard and insole screens.
Evaluate the bounding boxes of primary buttons (capture, accept, download) and assert each is at least 44px in both dimensions.
Screenshot a representative screen.

features.description: Desktop layouts are constrained to a readable max width and centered rather than stretching full-bleed.
steps: Set viewport to 1920x1080 and open the report page.
Assert the main content column has a max-width (content width well below 1920) and is horizontally centered.
Screenshot the desktop layout.

features.description: The app honors the OS preference: with prefers-color-scheme dark, the UI loads in dark theme automatically.
steps: Emulate prefers-color-scheme: dark and load the home page.
Assert the page background is dark and text is light (evaluate computed colors).
Screenshot the dark home page.

features.description: A theme toggle in the header switches between light and dark instantly across the whole app.
steps: Load the app in light mode.
Click the theme toggle in the header.
Assert the background/text colors flip to the dark palette without a reload.
Navigate to another route and assert dark mode persists.
Screenshot both themes.

features.description: The chosen theme persists across reloads via stored preference.
steps: Toggle the app to dark mode.
Reload the page.
Assert the app loads directly in dark mode with no light-mode flash content remaining.
Clear the preference, reload, and assert it falls back to the OS setting.

features.description: Report severity colors and annotations remain distinguishable and accessible in dark mode.
steps: Open the full report in dark mode.
Assert severity badges (normal/mild/marked) are visibly distinct against the dark background.
Assert the annotated capture image overlays remain readable.
Screenshot the dark-mode report.

features.description: The 3D viewer scene adapts to dark mode (background and lighting) so the insole stays clearly visible.
steps: Open the insole 3D viewer in light mode and screenshot.
Switch to dark mode.
Assert the viewer background darkens while the insole model remains well-lit and visible.
Screenshot the dark viewer.

features.description: Denying camera permission shows a helpful explainer with retry instructions instead of a broken screen.
steps: Launch the browser configured to deny camera permission.
Open the wizard capture step.
Assert a friendly message explains permission was denied and how to re-enable it in browser settings, with a 'Try again' button.
Screenshot the state.

features.description: When no pose is detected for several seconds, guidance appears telling the user to adjust framing/lighting.
steps: Open the capture step with a fake-camera video containing no person.
Wait ~5 seconds.
Assert an overlay message suggests stepping into frame with the full body visible.
Assert the capture button is disabled while no pose is detected.
Screenshot the guidance.

features.description: If the backend is unreachable when saving an analysis, a clear error with a Retry action appears and the capture is not lost.
steps: Simulate backend failure (block /api requests via network interception).
Accept a capture.
Assert an error toast/panel explains the save failed with a 'Retry' button, and the captured review remains on screen.
Restore the network, click Retry, and assert the save succeeds.
Screenshot the error state.

features.description: Invalid foot size input is rejected with a specific message and never sent to the backend.
steps: On the insole form enter 0, then letters, then 99.
Assert each case shows a specific inline validation error and the submit stays disabled.
Assert no insole generation network request was made (check network log).

features.description: STL generation failure shows an error card with retry, without breaking the rest of the result screen.
steps: Simulate a failing STL endpoint (intercept with a 500 response).
Click the STL download button.
Assert an error message appears offering retry, while the 3D viewer and recommendation remain functional.
Restore the endpoint, retry, and assert the download succeeds.
Screenshot the failure state.

features.description: Browsers without getUserMedia/WebGL support see a graceful capability notice naming supported browsers.
steps: Load the capture step with navigator.mediaDevices removed (evaluate/override before load).
Assert an unsupported-browser notice renders instead of the camera UI, listing modern supported browsers.
Screenshot the notice.

features.description: Accessing the app over insecure http shows a camera-blocked explanation and directs the user to the HTTPS (ngrok) link.
steps: Open the app over an insecure origin (or simulate isSecureContext=false).
Navigate to the capture step.
Assert the message explains cameras require HTTPS and shows/links the secure URL.
Screenshot the state.

features.description: Going offline mid-session shows an offline banner and queues user actions gracefully instead of throwing.
steps: Load the report page, then emulate browser offline mode.
Trigger an action requiring the network (e.g. accept a capture or regenerate the insole).
Assert an offline banner or toast appears with no unhandled console exceptions.
Re-enable the network and assert the banner clears.

features.description: If the pose model fails to load (CDN/wasm error), the capture screen shows a retriable model-load failure state.
steps: Intercept the MediaPipe model/wasm request to fail.
Open the capture step.
Assert a 'Pose engine failed to load' card renders with a Retry button and troubleshooting hint.
Restore the request, click Retry, and assert the skeleton overlay becomes functional.
Screenshot the failure card.

features.description: Before any capture, the wizard entry shows a friendly zero-state explaining the three views with an illustrated 'how it works'.
steps: Open the wizard in a fresh session.
Assert all three view cards show 'Not captured' and an intro explains the 3-view process.
Screenshot the zero-state.

features.description: Visiting the report page with no analysis shows an empty state with a CTA to start the posture scan, not a blank page.
steps: In a fresh session navigate directly to the report route.
Assert an illustrated empty state explains no analysis exists yet with a 'Start scan' button.
Click the button and assert the wizard opens.
Screenshot the empty state.

features.description: Visiting the insole area before completing the scan prompts the user to finish their posture analysis first, showing remaining views.
steps: In a session with only FRONT captured, navigate to the insole route.
Assert a gate message states the scan must be completed and lists SIDE and BACK as remaining, with a 'Continue scan' action.
Click it and assert the wizard resumes at the next incomplete view.
Screenshot the gate.

features.description: A partially completed report shows captured views normally and pending views as placeholder cards with capture shortcuts.
steps: Capture only the FRONT view, then open the full report.
Assert the FRONT section shows real metrics while SIDE and BACK render placeholder cards labeled 'Not captured yet' with capture buttons.
Click the SIDE placeholder button and assert the SIDE capture flow opens.
Screenshot the mixed report.

features.description: The entire wizard is operable by keyboard alone: tab to a view card, activate with Enter, trigger capture with the keyboard.
steps: Open the wizard entry and use only Tab/Enter to select the FRONT view.
Assert focus moves in a logical order and the capture screen opens.
Tab to the countdown capture button and press Enter; assert the countdown starts.
Assert Accept/Retake are reachable and operable via keyboard.

features.description: All interactive elements show a clearly visible focus indicator in both themes.
steps: Tab through the home page controls in light mode and assert each focused element shows a visible outline/ring.
Switch to dark mode and repeat, asserting the ring remains visible against dark surfaces.
Screenshot a focused control in each theme.

features.description: All meaningful images and captures have alt text; decorative icons are hidden from assistive tech.
steps: Open the report page.
Assert the capture thumbnails have descriptive alt attributes (e.g. 'Front view capture with posture annotations').
Assert purely decorative icons have aria-hidden or empty alt.
Assert the header logo exposes an accessible name.

features.description: Icon-only buttons (theme toggle, mirror, camera switch, reset view) expose aria-labels describing their action.
steps: Open the capture screen and the 3D viewer.
Assert each icon-only button has a non-empty aria-label matching its function.
Assert toggles expose state (aria-pressed or equivalent).

features.description: Body text and UI controls meet WCAG AA contrast in both light and dark themes.
steps: Open the home and report pages in light mode and compute contrast ratios of body text vs background; assert ≥ 4.5:1.
Repeat in dark mode.
Assert severity badges keep ≥ 3:1 contrast against their surroundings.
Screenshot evidence of both themes.

features.description: A skip-to-content link appears on first Tab press and jumps focus past the header to the main content.
steps: Load the home page and press Tab once.
Assert a 'Skip to content' link becomes visible as the first focusable element.
Press Enter and assert focus/scroll moves to the main content landmark.

features.description: Dynamic events — countdown ticks, capture taken, analysis saved — are announced via aria-live regions for screen reader users.
steps: Open the capture step and start a countdown capture.
Assert an aria-live region exists and its text content updates with the countdown and then a 'Capture complete' announcement.
Accept the capture and assert a save confirmation is announced in the live region.

features.description: The happy path (home → 3 captures → report → insole → STL) produces zero console errors.
steps: Run the full happy path with the fake camera while collecting console messages.
Assert no error-level console entries occurred at any step.
Save the console log summary as evidence.

features.description: User-facing error toasts include a short error reference ID that also appears in the console/log for correlation.
steps: Trigger a backend save failure (intercept /api).
Assert the error toast contains a reference code (e.g. 'ERR-XXXX').
Assert the same code is present in a console log entry.
Screenshot the toast.

features.description: An About/status panel shows frontend version, backend version, and pose model status fetched live.
steps: Open the About page.
Assert frontend version, backend API version (fetched from /api/health or /api/version), and pose model readiness are displayed.
Assert the backend value matches the API response (compare with a direct fetch).
Screenshot the panel.

features.description: A hidden debug panel (e.g. triple-tap the version or ?debug=1) reveals live pose FPS, landmark confidence, and last API latency.
steps: Load the capture step with '?debug=1' and the fake camera.
Assert a debug overlay shows FPS, average landmark confidence, and last API round-trip time, all updating.
Remove the flag and assert the overlay is hidden by default.
Screenshot the debug overlay.

features.description: The backend proxies AkashML (OpenAI-compatible) chat completions with the API key kept server-side.
steps: With AKASHML_API_KEY set, start the stack via ./init.sh.
From the browser, trigger a chat/agent action that calls the backend.
Assert a streamed AI response renders and that no API key is present in any network request the browser makes.
Screenshot the streamed response.

features.description: The agent calls real-time web tools via the Zero CLI (search → fetch, persisted agent session, no API key) and receives live data.
steps: Ensure the Zero CLI + agent session are set up (zero init; zero auth agent register).
Trigger an agent action that requires a live web lookup via `zero search`/`zero fetch`.
Assert the returned result contains fresh real-world data (a current price/listing), not a stub.
Screenshot the tool result in the UI.

features.description: Agent run state (runs and plans) is persisted in the app MySQL database and can be retrieved per session.
steps: Run a Concierge agent flow to completion.
Reload the page for the same session id.
Assert the prior agent run/plan is retrieved and shown (state persisted in MySQL).
Screenshot the restored run.

features.description: The backend agent orchestrator runs a bounded plan/tool-call/observe loop using the AkashML brain, Zero tools, and MySQL memory.
steps: Trigger the Concierge agent from an analysis result.
Assert the activity feed shows multiple sequential autonomous steps (plan then tool calls then result).
Assert the loop terminates with a structured plan and does not exceed its step cap.
Screenshot the completed run.

features.description: A one-click 'Get my fix' on the analysis result starts the Concierge agent with the findings and foot size and no further input.
steps: Launch Chromium with the fake camera (--use-file-for-fake-video-capture=tools/test_media/front.y4m).
Complete a posture analysis and open the result screen.
Click 'Get my fix' and provide no further input.
Assert the agent starts and begins streaming steps automatically.
Screenshot the agent starting.

features.description: The agent fetches real-time TPU/PETG filament price and availability via Zero web tools and shows it in the plan.
steps: Run the Concierge agent.
Assert the plan includes a filament section with a current price and availability sourced live.
Assert the value is labeled with its source/timestamp.
Screenshot the filament section.

features.description: The agent finds nearby 3D-print services or podiatrists using real-time web data and lists options.
steps: Run the Concierge agent (optionally with a location hint).
Assert the plan lists one or more real print-service/podiatrist options with links.
Screenshot the options list.

features.description: The agent researches evidence-based corrective exercises for the detected deficiencies and cites sources.
steps: Run the Concierge agent from a result containing at least one flagged deficiency.
Assert the plan includes tailored exercises that reference the specific deficiency.
Assert each recommendation shows a cited source link.
Screenshot the exercises section.

features.description: The agent compiles a personalized action plan plus a print/purchase quote and renders it to the user.
steps: Run the Concierge agent to completion.
Assert a consolidated action plan renders with a total estimated cost/quote.
Screenshot the final plan.

features.description: A live agent activity feed streams each autonomous step (SSE) so the user visibly sees the agent acting on the web without manual intervention.
steps: Trigger the Concierge agent.
Assert step entries appear incrementally (streamed), each naming the action/tool being used.
Assert no manual input is requested between steps.
Screenshot the streaming feed mid-run.

features.description: Agent memory/state is persisted to MySQL so a run is reviewable/resumable per session.
steps: Complete an agent run, then revisit the run from the session history.
Assert the full step history and plan are shown from persisted state.
Screenshot the reviewed run.

features.description: An agent tool failure, empty result, or timeout is handled gracefully with a partial plan and retry, not a crash.
steps: Trigger an agent run under a simulated tool failure (e.g. a tool returning no data).
Assert the UI shows a graceful message and any partial results, plus a retry affordance.
Assert the app does not crash and the console has no unhandled errors.
Screenshot the degraded state.

features.description: An agent run log (steps, tools used, tokens, estimated cost) is surfaced in the UI and saved under logs/.
steps: Complete an agent run.
Open the run details and assert steps, tools, token usage, and an estimated cost are shown.
Assert a corresponding log record exists under logs/.
Screenshot the run log.

features.description: The backend exposes an SSE chat proxy to AkashML for product Q&A with the API key server-side.
steps: Open the chatbot and send a product question.
Assert the answer streams token-by-token (SSE).
Assert no API key appears in browser network traffic.
Screenshot the streamed answer.

features.description: The chat widget is grounded in the product and refuses medical advice, pointing to the disclaimer.
steps: Ask the chatbot a product question and assert an on-topic grounded answer.
Ask a medical-diagnosis question (e.g. 'do I have a herniated disc?').
Assert it declines to diagnose and points to the not-medical disclaimer / a clinician.
Screenshot both responses.

features.description: The chatbot enforces rate limiting and token caps and shows a graceful error/429 state.
steps: Send messages rapidly past the configured per-session/IP limit.
Assert a friendly rate-limit message appears (not a raw error) once the limit is hit.
Screenshot the rate-limit state.

features.description: The landing page shows a hero with a single primary CTA ('Analyze my posture — free, 60s, nothing uploaded').
steps: Navigate to the landing page.
Assert the hero headline and a single prominent primary CTA are visible above the fold.
Click the CTA and assert it routes into the analysis flow.
Screenshot the hero.

features.description: A 'How it works' section explains the flow in three steps (Scan / See / Solve).
steps: Navigate to the landing page and scroll to How it works.
Assert three labeled steps (Scan, See, Solve) are visible with short copy.
Screenshot the section.

features.description: The landing page shows a privacy banner ('your video never leaves your device') and a visible wellness/not-medical disclaimer.
steps: Navigate to the landing page.
Assert a privacy statement that video/frames never leave the device is visible.
Assert a not-medical-advice wellness disclaimer is visible.
Screenshot both.

features.description: The landing page includes an interactive 3D insole teaser.
steps: Navigate to the landing page and scroll to the insole teaser.
Assert a 3D canvas renders and can be rotated (drag changes the view).
Screenshot the teaser.

features.description: The landing page shows persona cards (desk workers / runners / all-day standers) and a competitor comparison table.
steps: Navigate to the landing page.
Assert three persona cards are visible.
Assert a comparison table contrasts Newfoot with typical foot-scan competitors.
Screenshot both.

features.description: The landing page has an FAQ accordion, a testimonials/social-proof block, and a pricing/waitlist section.
steps: Navigate to the landing page.
Expand an FAQ item and assert its answer becomes visible.
Assert a testimonials block and a pricing/waitlist section are present.
Screenshot the sections.

features.description: On a mobile viewport the landing page is mobile-first and shows a sticky bottom CTA.
steps: Set a mobile viewport (e.g. 390x844) and open the landing page.
Assert the layout is single-column and readable without horizontal scroll.
Assert a sticky bottom CTA stays visible while scrolling.
Screenshot the mobile view.

features.description: An embedded AI chatbot widget is available on the landing page.
steps: Navigate to the landing page.
Assert a chat launcher is visible; open it.
Send a question and assert a streamed reply appears.
Screenshot the open widget.

features.description: A soft email gate appears after a few free chatbot/agent messages.
steps: Open the chatbot and send the configured number of free messages.
Assert an email-capture prompt appears before further messages are allowed.
Screenshot the gate.

features.description: The user can enter an email to receive the STL and action plan, and the lead is persisted keyed by session.
steps: From the result/plan screen, submit a valid email to receive the STL/plan.
Assert a confirmation is shown.
Assert the lead is persisted (retrievable for that session).
Screenshot the confirmation.

features.description: A returning visitor can retrieve their saved insole/plan via an emailed link, without a password or account.
steps: Open a saved-result link for an existing session.
Assert the previously generated insole/plan loads without any login step.
Screenshot the retrieved result.

features.description: Pomerium (open-source, identity-aware reverse proxy) runs as a docker-compose service in front of the app; all traffic routes through it.
steps: Start the stack with the Pomerium service enabled.
Open the app through the Pomerium route.
Assert the app loads via Pomerium (proxied) and health passes through it.
Screenshot the served app.

features.description: Optional: the dockerized stack is deployed to Akash Network (SDL + production frontend image) for a public HTTPS URL.
steps: Translate docker-compose to an Akash SDL and deploy via the Akash CLI/console.
Open the resulting public HTTPS URL and assert the app shell loads.
Assert /api/health returns ok through the deployment.
Screenshot the live deployed app.

features.description: Pose inference runs in a Web Worker (off the main thread) as a background daemon so the UI and skeleton overlay stay smooth while inference runs.
steps: Launch Chromium with the fake camera; open the live analysis screen.
Assert the skeleton overlay renders and the main thread stays responsive (no long-task jank while inference runs).
Assert (via a debug readout) that inference executes in a worker, not the main thread.
Screenshot the live overlay.

features.description: The perception pipeline runs as decoupled multi-rate loops (capture → inference → smoothing → metrics → feedback) so slow inference never blocks rendering.
steps: Open the live analysis screen with the fake camera.
Assert a debug/perf panel shows separate rates for capture (~30Hz), inference, and metrics loops.
Assert the overlay render rate stays high even when the inference rate is lower.
Screenshot the perf panel.

features.description: A One-Euro temporal filter smooths each landmark so the skeleton overlay is visibly stable (jitter removed) compared with raw detections.
steps: Unit-test the One-Euro filter on a noisy landmark fixture and assert output variance drops without added lag beyond tolerance.
Open the live screen and toggle raw vs smoothed; assert the smoothed skeleton is visibly steadier.
Screenshot both states.

features.description: Low-visibility landmarks are confidence-weighted (attenuated or interpolated) so occluded joints do not produce aberrant angles.
steps: Unit-test that landmarks below a visibility threshold are down-weighted/interpolated in the smoothing stage.
Open the live screen; assert low-confidence joints render de-emphasized (per F038) and their metrics are marked low-confidence rather than wrong.
Screenshot the de-emphasized state.

features.description: Deficiencies are persistence-gated: a metric must stay past its threshold for a sustained window (with hysteresis) before being flagged, so transient movement does not trigger false findings.
steps: Unit-test the persistence/hysteresis gate: a brief threshold breach does NOT flag; a sustained one does.
On the live screen, move briefly out of alignment and assert no finding is raised.
Hold a deviation past the window and assert the finding then appears.
Screenshot the flagged state.

features.description: A temporal sequence model over a rolling window classifies posture stability/state (the article's LSTM approach; baseline = rolling stats, upgrade = TF.js LSTM), improving robustness over single-frame detection.
steps: Unit-test the temporal classifier on a sequence fixture (stable vs unstable) and assert correct labels.
On the live screen, assert a posture-state indicator reflects sustained state, not per-frame flicker.
Screenshot the indicator.

features.description: Closed-loop live coaching guides the user ("step back", "hold still", "aligned") with a real-time posture-quality/confidence meter driven by the daemon.
steps: Open the live analysis screen with the fake camera.
Assert contextual coaching messages appear and update as the daemon state changes.
Assert a live quality/confidence meter is visible and moves with detection quality.
Screenshot the coaching + meter.

features.description: Adaptive calibration estimates camera tilt, scale (inter-shoulder distance) and the user's neutral baseline so thresholds normalize per setup.
steps: Unit-test the calibration math (tilt from a vertical reference; scale from inter-shoulder pixels).
On the live screen, assert a calibration status shows once a stable baseline is captured.
Assert metrics use the calibrated scale (a debug readout shows normalized values).
Screenshot the calibrated state.

features.description: The daemon logs only derived metrics (never video) for offline threshold/filter tuning via the run_loop self-improvement hook — privacy-preserving.
steps: Run a live session and assert a derived-metrics record is produced (angles/stats only, no image data).
Assert no frame/image bytes are present in the logged payload or any network request.
Screenshot the metrics record.

features.description: A Pomerium access policy protects a restricted route (e.g. a clinician/admin view): allowed identities pass; others get a friendly access-denied page.
steps: Configure a Pomerium policy allowing a specific identity to a protected route.
Access the protected route as an allowed identity and assert it loads.
Access it as a disallowed/anonymous identity and assert a clear access-denied response.
Screenshot both outcomes.

features.description: Pomerium terminates HTTPS and passes identity headers to the backend, acting as the secure front door (satisfying the camera secure-context).
steps: Open the app through Pomerium over HTTPS.
Assert the connection is secure (TLS) and the camera secure-context is satisfied.
Assert the backend receives Pomerium identity headers on a protected call.
Screenshot the secure app + a header readout.
